programmes.show_description: Single mother Sandra embarks on an affair with an old friend. Contains some strong language and some scenes of a sexual nature. [2022]
5/6. Seeking answers, Carey and Pierson are pursued through a network of underground tunnels. Contains strong language, some violence and upsetting scenes.
Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.
Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor visit Bisterne Estate in Hampshire's Avon Valley.
2/26. Fifty years on from a fire at Inveraray Castle, Dougie finds out how it was rescued.
2/26. Beechgrove Garden is looking ahead to summer days in the garden, with Lizzie Schofield back to work in her sitooterie area.
The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.
A mouthwatering menu from Anna Jones, Joe Wicks, Sabrina Ghayour and Georgina Hayden. Also, Rick Stein, Galton Blackiston and Clive Myrie head out of the studio.
20/29. The remaining three chefs from London and south east England compete over mains and desserts inspired by the British film industry. Who will win, and who must go home?
21/29. It's judging day, and only one chef can go through to represent London and south east England. Much-loved actor Simon Callow helps decide the winner from the two remaining chefs.
The British Motor Museum plays host to this programme, where experts Philip Serrell and Richard Madley help the teams find bargains to sell for profit at auction.

programmes.show_name: Pilgrimage: The Road to Holy Island
programmes.show_description: 3/3. The pilgrims are in the Scottish Borders on St Cuthbert's Way in the Cheviots, reaching spectacular views on Wideopen Hill.
Suspense thriller about a recuperating photojournalist who passes the time by watching the neighbours from the confines of his own apartment and believes he has witnessed a murder. [1954]
The Ulster Championship opens with Armagh v Tyrone, presented by Mark Sidebottom, with Sarah Mulkerrins pitch side. Commentary from Thomas Niblock & All Star, Conor McManus.
Sheep are indoors for the winter, while Kerry and his researchers set about tagging oyster catchers. And boxes are being erected for barn owls.
1/4. Paula shares recipes for cullen skink and a delicious baked apple pudding. She heads to Cushendun beach, where she shows two young fiddle players how to barbecue pork shoulder.
A glittering celebration with Nick Mohammed, plus performances from Paddington and Evita. Contains scenes of flashing images.

programmes.show_name: Hell for Leather:  The Story of Gaelic Football
programmes.show_description: 1/4. Through rebellion and civil war, Gaelic football explodes like a prairie fire to become the most popular sport in Ireland. Contains some strong language.

programmes.show_name: The Good, the Bad and the Ugly
programmes.show_description: Two unlikely partners in a bounty-hunting scam join forces in a new venture. Contains some violence and some upsetting scenes. [1968]
Police hunt for a man who has violently sexually attacked a woman in a public phone box. Contains scenes which some viewers may find disturbing.
3/6. Flashbacks reveal Stephen and Lisa's first meeting. Amanda hides her pregnancy. Contains strong language and scenes which some viewers may find upsetting.
4/6. At a wedding, Lisa deals with Amanda's pregnancy, while Stephen seeks to confront Dave. Contains strong language and scenes which some viewers may find upsetting.
Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.
Eric Knowles hitches a lift with a host of experts and their red and blue teams as they journey to antiques fairs in search of car-related collectibles to sell at auction.
1/5. David Attenborough reveals the lives of kingfishers, otters and other animals whose home is a river garden. Life here seems idyllic, but all are at the mercy of seasonal floods.
Gabby Logan presents highlights from Sunday's four Premier League games, including title contenders Manchester City against Chelsea.
Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.
The big political issues of the day.
6/30. Game show in which players score points by answering questions correctly while avoiding the impossible answers.
19/25. In the ultimate knockout quiz, eight players do battle on the Tournament Run in a series of exciting tug-of-war quiz matches. Hosted by Alex Scott.
9/25. Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts the quick-fire quiz where six plucky contestants must stay out of the light to be alright. Can anyone beat the lightning and win the £3,000 jackpot?
...Adventure: Kingston - Jamaica. 2/15. Clive discovers a new talent for dancehall moves in Jamaica's vibrant capital city.
2/3. Nature documentary series. Liz Bonnin leads a team using cutting-edge technology to follow caribou on a 5,000km migration through the Arctic wilderness of Canada and Alaska.
4/26. Michael Aspel invites members of the public to bring along their antiques for examination. Treasures from Hartlepool include a Steiff teddy bear and a drug jar.
Flog it! comes from Chiddingstone Castle in Kent. Antiques experts Thomas Plant and Adam Partridge find some fascinating antiques and collectibles to take to auction.
11/100. Jacqui Joseph, Ben Thompson, Laura Tobin and Henning Wehn test their general knowledge skills on this week's House of Games.
1/15. Beginning his journey in Osaka, Michael explores the artistic phenomenon of manga, takes a trip on the Shinkansen and, in Nagoya, visits the factory making the smart loo.
Farhad Javed reports on Pakistan's role in the US-Iran ceasefire, and we hear how routine visits for medical treatment in Pakistan have led to children becoming infected with HIV.

36/37. The second set of semi-finalists face off for a place in the final. Amol Rajan asks the questions.

1/4. Clodagh loves to shop, especially for handbags. Can she be inspired to wear the clothes she already owns and find out more about sustainable handbag options?
Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Victoria Derbyshire.   Followed by Weather.
1/3. A single mum from Scotland embarks on a whirlwind romance with a Ghanaian preacher. Contains some scenes which some viewers may find upsetting.
1/8. Bob, Paul and Ted are targeting tench, and Bob makes Paul's dreams come true when he reveals their overnight accommodation.
